events.summary: Sanofi made an unsolicited approach and requested a telephone discussion with Medivation.
Medivation identified J. P. Morgan as retained as its independent financial advisor at the March 24 special meeting.
notes: The filing ties the mandate to routine defense preparedness and general strategic advice.

events.summary: Sanofi submitted a non-binding proposal to acquire Medivation for $52.50 per share in cash.
notes: The filing states the letter was received on April 15, 2016 but dated April 13, 2016.

events.summary: Pfizer indicated interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
Medivation engaged Evercore as an additional independent financial advisor.
Sanofi publicly announced its proposal in a press release.
Medivation had retained Wachtell, Lipton, Rosen & Katz as an additional legal advisor by the April 28 special meeting.
Pfizer reiterated its interest in participating in any strategic discussions Medivation might initiate.
Sanofi restated its proposal in a press release and threatened a consent solicitation.
Sanofi sought a confidentiality agreement and due diligence access for a potential strategic transaction.
notes: Sanofi said it could pay more but did not indicate how much more.

events.summary: Pfizer reiterated interest in a negotiated transaction process with Medivation.
Medivation's board launched a controlled strategic process and directed J. P. Morgan and Evercore to contact select industry participants.
Sanofi submitted an increased proposal of $58.00 per share in cash plus a CVR of up to $3.00 per share.
notes: The CVR was conditioned on certain revenue metrics.

events.summary: Pfizer entered into a confidentiality agreement with Medivation.
notes: The same block also references one additional unnamed party that is not linkable to a locked actor_id.

events.summary: Sanofi entered into a confidentiality and standstill agreement with Medivation.
notes: Sanofi agreed to terminate its Consent Solicitation as part of the agreement.

events.summary: Pfizer's management team expressed continued interest in exploring a negotiated strategic transaction with Medivation.
Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with a fourth industry participant initially contacted by its financial advisors.
notes: The filing later relabels this counterparty as one of Company 1-4 but does not identify which specific actor corresponds to the July 9 execution date.

date.resolution_note: Year inferred from the surrounding July 2016 chronology.

events.summary: Medivation entered into a confidentiality agreement with a fifth industry participant initially contacted by its financial advisors.
notes: The filing later relabels this counterparty as one of Company 1-4 but does not identify which specific actor corresponds to the July 13 execution date.

events.summary: Pfizer submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal for an all-cash acquisition at $65.00 per share.
Company 1 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal for an all-cash acquisition at $71.00 per share.
Company 2 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal for an all-cash acquisition at $62.00 to $64.00 per share.
Company 3 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal of $60.00 per share in cash plus CVRs of up to $10.00 per share.
notes: The CVRs were conditioned on certain revenue metrics.

events.summary: Company 4 submitted a non-binding preliminary proposal of $63.00 per share in cash plus CVRs of up to $5.00 per share.
notes: The CVRs were conditioned on certain regulatory milestones for pipeline assets.

events.summary: Company 2 communicated by telephone that it would submit a revised all-cash proposal at $70.00 per share.
notes: The proposal was communicated by telephone.

events.summary: Company 2 submitted a written revised all-cash proposal at $70.00 per share.
Company 3 submitted a written revised all-cash proposal at $70.50 per share.
J. P. Morgan and Evercore sent a written instruction letter requiring definitive written proposals from the Interested Parties by August 19, 2016.
notes: The instructions also requested merger agreement comments by August 18, 2016.

events.summary: Company 3 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $72.50 per share.
notes: Company 3 also submitted a revised version of the merger agreement.

events.summary: Company 1 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $73.00 per share.
notes: Company 1 also submitted a revised version of the merger agreement.

events.summary: Company 2 submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $75.50 per share.
Pfizer submitted a definitive all-cash proposal at $77.00 per share.
The August 19 deadline for definitive written proposals arrived and definitive proposals were submitted.
Medivation's financial advisors told the remaining Interested Parties that best and final proposals were due by noon on August 20, 2016.

deadline_date.raw_text: August 20, 2016, by 12:00 p. m. Pacific Time

notes: The best and final submissions were required to confirm that no further diligence would be required.

events.summary: Pfizer submitted a revised final proposal at $81.50 per share.
notes: The final proposal confirmed that it was not subject to further diligence and included a revised draft of the merger agreement.

events.summary: Company 1 submitted a revised final proposal at $80.25 per share.
notes: The final proposal confirmed that it was not subject to further diligence and included a revised draft of the merger agreement.

events.summary: Company 2 submitted a revised final proposal at $80.00 per share.
notes: The final proposal confirmed that it was not subject to further diligence and included a revised draft of the merger agreement.

events.summary: The August 20 noon deadline for best and final proposals arrived and final proposals were submitted.
Medivation executed the merger agreement with Pfizer.
notes: Medivation also executed the Rights Amendment the same day.

events.summary: The parties issued a joint press release announcing the transaction.

exclusions.explanation: The filing references additional unnamed industry participants and a twelfth contacted party that cannot be linked confidently to specific actor_ids in the locked roster.
The filing later relabels the four non-Pfizer NDA counterparties as Company 1-4 but does not identify which specific Company 1-4 actor corresponds to the June 29, July 9, or July 13 execution dates.
